acceptanceCriteria: Deterministic triggers cover high or critical risk, low confidence, repeated unchanged failure, security, data integrity, and explicit human request
Transmission denial is evaluated before any review request and yields Claude-only continuation without invocation
Unavailable plugin distinguishes optional skip, required stop, and Claude-only continuation
Structured findings are bounded, repository-relative, versioned, and preserve unavailable metadata without inference
Existing consultation mutation checks remain mandatory and Codex results never create verification or approval
Fixture tests need no live Codex account and required repository checks pass
constraints: No external transmission or live Codex invocation
No overwrite, reset, checkout, commit, push, or PR creation
Preserve v1 artifact semantics through additive versioning
Do not persist absolute paths, transcripts, chain of thought, secrets, or unnecessary command output
Do not infer model, effort, usage, tokens, or cost metadata
stopConditions: a required check fails twice without a new hypothesis
